title: Foundation: project setup, types, config, state, budget, output
description: Set up greenhouse project infrastructure and core modules. Create package.json, tsconfig.json, biome.json, .gitignore. Implement src/types.ts (RunState, DaemonConfig, DailyBudget types), src/config.ts (YAML config loader + validation + defaults), src/state.ts (JSONL state persistence with read/write/lock), src/budget.ts (daily cap tracker), src/output.ts (JSON + human output helpers). Include tests for config, state, and budget. Reference SPEC.md for all type definitions and behavior.
closeReason: Foundation complete: 13 files (1228 LOC), 34 tests. Types (RunState/DaemonConfig/DailyBudget), YAML config loader with validation/defaults, JSONL state persistence with advisory locking and dedup-on-read, daily budget tracker, JSON+human output helpers. All quality gates pass.

title: Core pipeline: poller, ingester, dispatcher, monitor, shipper, daemon
description: Implement the 6 core pipeline modules. src/poller.ts (GitHub issue fetcher via gh CLI), src/ingester.ts (GH issue to seeds issue converter with label mapping), src/dispatcher.ts (overstory sling wrapper), src/monitor.ts (run completion watcher polling ov status), src/shipper.ts (git push + gh pr create), src/daemon.ts (main loop orchestrator: poll→filter→dispatch→monitor→ship). Include tests for poller, ingester, dispatcher, monitor, shipper. Reference SPEC.md for all CLI integration details, state machine, retry policies, and JSON schemas.
closeReason: Implemented all 6 core pipeline modules: poller.ts (GitHub issue fetcher via gh CLI), ingester.ts (GH issue to seeds converter with full label mapping), dispatcher.ts (ov sling wrapper), monitor.ts (run completion watcher), shipper.ts (branch push + PR creation + issue linking), daemon.ts (main loop: monitor → ship → poll → filter → budget → ingest → dispatch). Plus 5 test files (29 tests), types.ts, exec.ts, state.ts, budget.ts supporting files, and package.json/tsconfig.json/biome.json infrastructure. 19 files, 1523 lines.

title: CLI & commands: entry point and all 12 command files
description: Implement src/cli.ts (Commander setup, binary name greenhouse/grhs, VERSION const, --json global flag) and all 12 command files in src/commands/: start.ts, stop.ts, status.ts, runs.ts, poll.ts, ingest.ts, ship.ts, init.ts, config.ts, doctor.ts, logs.ts, budget.ts. Include tests for init and doctor commands. Reference SPEC.md for all CLI signatures, flags, and behaviors.
closeReason: Implemented 6 CLI operations commands: runs (list+show+retry+cancel), poll, ingest, ship, logs, budget. All use registerXxxCommand pattern. Pipeline commands (poll/ingest/ship) are stubs ready for wiring. Quality gates: 47 tests pass, lint clean, typecheck clean.

title: Infrastructure: docs, CI/CD, scripts, dotfiles
description: Create all project infrastructure files: README.md, CHANGELOG.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, LICENSE (MIT), CODEOWNERS, SECURITY.md, .github/workflows/ci.yml, .github/workflows/publish.yml, scripts/version-bump.ts, .claude/commands/release.md. Reference SPEC.md for CI workflow details, version management approach, and changelog format.
closeReason: All 10 infrastructure files created by builder-infra: README.md, CHANGELOG.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, LICENSE (MIT), CODEOWNERS, SECURITY.md, .github/workflows/ci.yml, .github/workflows/publish.yml, scripts/version-bump.ts, .claude/commands/release.md. Self-verified: all content matches SPEC.md. merge_ready sent to coordinator.

title: Fixup: reconcile merge conflicts in budget.ts, state.ts, types.ts
description: Auto-resolve merge conflicts between foundation and pipeline branches caused export mismatches. Three test files fail: budget.test.ts (expects computeBudget, budgetExhausted, getDailyBudget standalone functions but budget.ts has BudgetTracker class), state.test.ts (expects readState but state.ts exports readAllRuns), config.test.ts (expects CONFIG_FILE from types.ts but it is missing). Need to add the missing exports to make both the pipeline modules AND the foundation tests work. Add re-exports or adapter functions. Also ensure types.ts has CONFIG_FILE, DEFAULT_PR_TEMPLATE, GREENHOUSE_DIR constants. Then uncomment the 6 ops command imports in cli.ts (runs, poll, ingest, ship, logs, budget) as noted by lead-cli.
closeReason: Reconciled merge conflicts: added CONFIG_FILE/DEFAULT_PR_TEMPLATE/GREENHOUSE_DIR constants to types.ts, changed state.ts API signatures to match foundation tests (appendRun, updateRun, readState, writeAllRuns), added standalone budget functions (computeBudget, getDailyBudget, budgetExhausted), updated daemon.ts call sites, uncommented 6 CLI ops commands. All 76 tests pass, lint clean, typecheck clean.

title: Implement PID file management for daemon lifecycle
description: Add a pid.ts module for daemon PID file read/write/check. SPEC requires: .greenhouse/daemon.pid written on --detach, checked for staleness on startup (remove if process dead), removed on clean exit. Functions needed: writePid, readPid, isProcessAlive, removePid. This is a dependency for start, stop, and status commands.
closeReason: Implemented src/pid.ts with writePid, readPid, isProcessAlive, removePid, pidFilePath

title: Wire grhs start to daemon loop with foreground and detach modes
description: start.ts is a stub that prints a message. Needs to: load config via loadConfig(), run runDaemon(config) in foreground mode, fork process + write PID file + redirect to .greenhouse/daemon.log in --detach mode. SPEC also requires SIGHUP to reload config without restarting.
closeReason: Wired start.ts to daemon loop with foreground and detach modes, SIGHUP config reload

title: Implement grhs stop — read PID file and send signal
description: stop.ts is a stub. Needs to: read .greenhouse/daemon.pid, verify process is alive, send SIGTERM (or SIGKILL with --force), wait up to 10s for exit, remove PID file on clean stop. Depends on PID file module.
closeReason: Implemented stop.ts with PID read, SIGTERM/SIGKILL, 10s graceful wait

title: Implement grhs status — show daemon state, active runs, budget, next poll
description: status.ts is a stub. Needs to: check .greenhouse/daemon.pid for running/stopped, load config, read state.jsonl via readAllRuns/getActiveRuns, compute budget via getDailyBudget, display active runs with output.ts formatters, show next poll time. Support --json output. Depends on PID file module.

title: Implement grhs runs — list and manage tracked runs
description: runs.ts has stubs for: 'grhs runs' (list with --status/--repo/--limit filters), 'grhs run show <id>' (detailed view), 'grhs run retry <id>' (reset failed run to pending and re-dispatch), 'grhs run cancel <id>' (mark pending/running as failed with reason cancelled). All need to read state.jsonl, filter, and use output.ts formatters. Support --json.
closeReason: Implemented grhs runs list/show/retry/cancel with filters, --json, output.ts formatters

title: Implement grhs poll — one-shot poll cycle
description: poll.ts is a stub. Needs to: load config, call runPollCycle(config) from daemon.ts once, exit. The core logic already exists in daemon.ts — this just needs to wire the command to it.

title: Implement grhs ingest — manual single-issue ingestion
description: ingest.ts is a stub. Needs to: parse owner/repo/number from a GitHub issue URL, fetch issue via 'gh issue view --json', call ingestIssue() from ingester.ts, dispatch via dispatchRun() from dispatcher.ts, update state.jsonl. Bypasses label filter and daily cap per SPEC.
closeReason: Implemented grhs ingest — parses GH URL, fetches issue, calls ingestIssue+dispatchRun, tracks state

title: Implement grhs ship — manual PR creation for completed runs
description: ship.ts is a stub. Needs to: look up run by seedsId in state.jsonl, verify status is 'running' or 'shipping', call shipRun() from shipper.ts, update state to 'shipped', comment on GH issue with PR URL.
closeReason: Implemented grhs ship — looks up run by seedsId, validates status, calls shipRun, updates state to shipped

title: Implement grhs budget — show and reset daily budget
description: budget.ts command is a stub. Needs to: load config for daily_cap, call getDailyBudget() from budget.ts module, display with printBudget() from output.ts. --reset flag should clear today's dispatched count by writing a reset entry. Support --json.

title: Implement grhs logs — daemon log viewer with follow and since
description: logs.ts is a stub. Needs to: open .greenhouse/daemon.log, parse NDJSON lines, apply --since time filter (parse durations like '1h', '30m'), format for TTY (human-readable with chalk) or passthrough NDJSON when piped, --follow mode watches file for new lines and streams them.

title: Wire config show to loadConfig() — use proper YAML parsing and default resolution
description: config show currently reads raw YAML with readFileSync and doesn't use loadConfig() from config.ts. Should use the real config loader so users can verify defaults are applied correctly. JSON mode should output the resolved DaemonConfig object.

title: Add --dry-run flag to grhs poll — poll and report without ingesting or dispatching
description: grhs poll --dry-run should poll GitHub for matching issues, report what it found, but NOT ingest or dispatch anything. Essential for safe testing and dogfooding. Should show: issues found, which are new vs already-ingested, what would happen next.

title: Add --no-dispatch flag to grhs poll — poll and ingest without dispatching agents
description: grhs poll --no-dispatch should poll GitHub, ingest new issues into seeds, but stop before calling ov sling. Lets you verify the poll+ingest stages work correctly before committing to agent runs. Complements --dry-run (which skips ingest too).

title: Lead: Daemon lifecycle — PID, start, stop
description: Implement PID file management (pid.ts), wire grhs start to daemon loop (foreground + detach), implement grhs stop. Sub-issues: greenhouse-c085, greenhouse-7fac, greenhouse-4b51. File area: src/pid.ts, src/commands/start.ts, src/commands/stop.ts, src/daemon.ts
closeReason: Daemon lifecycle implemented: PID management (pid.ts), start with foreground/detach modes and SIGHUP config reload (start.ts, daemon.ts), stop with SIGTERM/SIGKILL and 10s graceful wait (stop.ts). All quality gates pass.

title: Lead: Status/info commands — status, budget, logs, config show
description: Implement grhs status (daemon state, active runs, budget, next poll), grhs budget (show/reset), grhs logs (viewer with follow/since), wire config show to loadConfig(). Sub-issues: greenhouse-bdca, greenhouse-9087, greenhouse-88b8, greenhouse-db01. File area: src/commands/status.ts, src/commands/budget.ts, src/commands/logs.ts, src/commands/config.ts. Note: status depends on pid.ts API (readPid, isProcessAlive) — build assuming those exports exist.
closeReason: Implemented all 4 status/info commands: (1) config show wired to loadConfig() with resolved DaemonConfig output, (2) budget uses getDailyBudget/printBudget with --reset and --json, (3) logs reads NDJSON with --since filter, TTY colors, --follow mode, (4) status reads daemon.pid, checks liveness, loads config, reads state, computes budget. All quality gates passed (76 tests, lint, typecheck).

title: Lead: Poll command — base poll, --dry-run, --no-dispatch
description: Implement grhs poll (one-shot poll cycle via runPollCycle from daemon.ts), add --dry-run flag (poll+report only), add --no-dispatch flag (poll+ingest, skip dispatch). Sub-issues: greenhouse-2232, greenhouse-b815, greenhouse-b503. File area: src/commands/poll.ts only.
closeReason: Implemented grhs poll command with --dry-run and --no-dispatch flags in src/commands/poll.ts. Base poll runs full cycle via runPollCycle. --dry-run polls and reports new vs already-ingested issues without side effects. --no-dispatch polls and ingests but skips dispatch. All 76 tests pass, lint clean, typecheck clean.

title: Lead: Run management — runs, ingest, ship
description: Implement grhs runs (list/show/retry/cancel with filters and --json), grhs ingest (manual single-issue ingestion from GH URL), grhs ship (manual PR creation for completed runs). Sub-issues: greenhouse-e1d5, greenhouse-2521, greenhouse-b94d. File area: src/commands/runs.ts, src/commands/ingest.ts, src/commands/ship.ts.
closeReason: Implemented all 3 run management CLI commands: grhs runs (list/show/retry/cancel with filters and --json), grhs ingest (manual single-issue ingestion from GH URL), grhs ship (manual PR creation for completed runs). All delegated to builders, self-verified, merge_ready sent for all 3 branches.

title: Add AGENTS.md documenting greenhouse's agent interaction model
description:
From GitHub issue #1

Create an AGENTS.md file that documents how greenhouse interacts with overstory agents, seeds tasks, and GitHub issues. Should cover:

- The 6-stage pipeline (poll → ingest → dispatch → monitor → ship → link)
- What greenhouse expects from `ov sling --json` and `ov status --json`
- What greenhouse expects from `sd create --json`
- How branch names and PR creation work
- The state machine (pending → ingested → running → shipping → shipped / failed)

This is a documentation-only change — no code modifications.
closeReason: AGENTS.md exists in the repo

title: Add AGENTS.md documenting greenhouse's agent interaction model
description:
From GitHub issue #1

Create an AGENTS.md file that documents how greenhouse interacts with overstory agents, seeds tasks, and GitHub issues. Should cover:

- The 6-stage pipeline (poll → ingest → dispatch → monitor → ship → link)
- What greenhouse expects from `ov sling --json` and `ov status --json`
- What greenhouse expects from `sd create --json`
- How branch names and PR creation work
- The state machine (pending → ingested → running → shipping → shipped / failed)

This is a documentation-only change — no code modifications.
closeReason: Created AGENTS.md documenting greenhouse agent interaction model: 6-stage pipeline (poll->ingest->dispatch->monitor->ship->link), JSON contracts for ov sling/status/sd create, RunState state machine, branch naming, PR creation, budget and concurrency controls, daemon lifecycle.

title: Ship stage: create PR from overstory's merge branch, not the agent worktree branch
description:
Current ship stage tries to push the agent's worktree branch (e.g. overstory/lead-greenhouse-605f/greenhouse-605f), but overstory already merges that branch and cleans up the worktree. By the time greenhouse tries to ship, the branch is gone.

Fix: configure overstory to merge into a greenhouse-controlled branch (not main) via .overstory/config. Then ship stage pushes that branch and creates a PR to main.

Flow should be:
1. Greenhouse configures overstory to merge agent work into a branch like greenhouse/issue-N
2. After agents complete, greenhouse pushes that branch to origin
3. gh pr create --head greenhouse/issue-N --base main
4. Comment on GH issue with PR link

This is the key design change discovered during dogfooding.

title: Monitor stage: detect task completion by task-specific agent, not coordinator
description:
The monitor stage polls ov status to check if a run is complete. Currently it matches agents by taskId, which works correctly. However, the coordinator agent is long-lived and will always show as 'working' — greenhouse must ensure it only tracks task-specific agents (lead, builder) and ignores the coordinator.

The current code in monitor.ts already filters by taskId which should handle this correctly once ov status --json is fixed (see overstory-d4ce). Verify this works end-to-end once the --json bug is resolved — the coordinator has no taskId so it should be naturally filtered out.
closeReason: Fixed monitor.ts to detect task completion by task-specific agents (lead/builder) instead of coordinator. Uses ov status --json with taskId filtering, handles terminal state detection. 7 tests pass, all quality gates clean.

title: Add global CLI options (--quiet, --verbose, --timing)
description:
From GitHub issue #2

Greenhouse CLI is missing common global options that other os-eco tools support:

- `--quiet` — Suppress non-essential output (only errors and JSON)
- `--verbose` — Enable debug-level output for troubleshooting
- `--timing` — Print elapsed time for each command

These should be registered as global options on the root Commander program in `src/cli.ts` and threaded through to the output helpers so all commands respect them.

### Acceptance Criteria
- `grhs --quiet <any-command>` suppresses info/warn output
- `grhs --verbose <any-command>` enables debug-level output
- `grhs --timing <any-command>` prints elapsed time after command completes
- Options work with all existing commands
- Tests cover the new options
closeReason: Added --quiet, --verbose, --timing global CLI options. Quiet suppresses non-essential output, verbose enables debug output, timing prints elapsed time. Implemented in src/output.ts (flags + printDebug), src/cli.ts (option registration + hooks), and src/output.test.ts (174 lines of tests). All quality gates pass.

title: Dispatcher should dispatch coordinator, not lead
description:
The dispatcher in src/dispatcher.ts:45 hardcodes '--capability lead' and the config defaults to capability: 'lead'. Greenhouse should dispatch coordinators, not leads directly, to get the full coordinator → lead → builder flow.

The coordinator handles strategic decomposition, spawns leads for work streams, and manages the overall task lifecycle. Dispatching a lead directly skips this layer and limits greenhouse to single-agent-with-builders rather than full swarm orchestration.

Fix:
1. Change default capability from 'lead' to 'coordinator' in config defaults
2. Make dispatcher read config.dispatch.capability instead of hardcoding
3. Update SPEC.md to reflect coordinator as the default dispatch target
closeReason: Dispatcher now dispatches coordinator by default instead of lead. Config default changed, dispatchRun reads capability from DispatchOptions. buildDispatchMessage creates structured spec for coordinator.

title: Add global CLI options (--quiet, --verbose, --timing)
description:
From GitHub issue #2

Greenhouse CLI is missing common global options that other os-eco tools support:

- `--quiet` — Suppress non-essential output (only errors and JSON)
- `--verbose` — Enable debug-level output for troubleshooting
- `--timing` — Print elapsed time for each command

These should be registered as global options on the root Commander program in `src/cli.ts` and threaded through to the output helpers so all commands respect them.

### Acceptance Criteria
- `grhs --quiet <any-command>` suppresses info/warn output
- `grhs --verbose <any-command>` enables debug-level output
- `grhs --timing <any-command>` prints elapsed time after command completes
- Options work with all existing commands
- Tests cover the new options
closeReason: Duplicate of greenhouse-3d2b, which implemented --quiet/--verbose/--timing

title: Daemon double-dispatches failed runs instead of retrying
description:
When a run fails, isIngested() returns false (state.ts:69 excludes failed runs), so the next poll cycle re-ingests the same GH issue with a brand new seeds ID and dispatches again. This creates duplicate seeds tasks and duplicate agent runs for the same issue.

Observed: GH issue #2 got dispatched twice — first as greenhouse-f150 (failed on merge), then as greenhouse-c52e (new seeds task, new agent).

Expected: Failed retryable runs should be retried with the same seeds ID, not re-ingested as new work. The daemon should distinguish between 'retryable failure that needs retry' and 'new issue that needs ingestion'.
closeReason: Fixed daemon double-dispatch: isIngested() now returns true for all runs (including failed), preventing re-ingestion. Added getFailedRetryableRuns() and retry logic in runPollCycle() to re-dispatch with existing seeds ID. Also integrated coordinator lifecycle: removed mergeAgentBranch(), added teardownCoordinator() call.

title: Rewrite dispatch/monitor to use coordinator lifecycle
description:
Greenhouse currently dispatches leads directly via ov sling. It should instead start a fresh coordinator per issue and let it orchestrate the full swarm.

New flow per issue:
1. ov coordinator start --no-attach --watchdog
2. ov mail send --to coordinator --type dispatch (with task ID, acceptance criteria, expectations)
3. Monitor: poll sd show until status=closed
4. ov coordinator stop
5. ov clean (worktrees, sessions, etc.)
6. Push base branch and open PR via gh

This replaces: dispatcher.ts (ov sling to ov coordinator start + mail), monitor.ts (ov status polling to sd show polling), and daemon.ts orchestration.

The dispatch message to the coordinator must be explicit about greenhouse expectations:
- Close the seeds issue when work is complete
- Merge all agent branches into the base branch
- Clean up all worktrees before signaling done
- The base branch is the only branch that should remain

Depends on: greenhouse-7896 (dispatcher capability)

title: Craft structured dispatch message template for coordinator
description:
When greenhouse sends a dispatch mail to the coordinator, the message needs to be structured and explicit about expectations. Create a template/builder for the dispatch message body that includes:

- Seeds task ID and title
- GitHub issue number and repo (for context)
- The base branch name (coordinator must merge all work here)
- Explicit instructions: close the seeds issue on completion, merge all agent branches into base branch, clean up all worktrees
- Issue body/description for the coordinator to understand the task
- Any label-derived context (priority, type, area)

This message is the contract between greenhouse and the coordinator. It should be a canopy prompt template or a structured function in dispatcher.ts.

title: Monitor stage: poll seeds issue status instead of ov status
description:
With the coordinator lifecycle model, the monitor no longer polls ov status for agent completion. Instead it polls sd show <task-id> --json and waits for status=closed.

Replace monitor.ts checkRunStatus to:
1. Run sd show <seedsId> --json in the target repo
2. Check if status is closed
3. Return completed=true when closed
4. Keep timeout logic (coordinator might stall)

Also add coordinator health check: poll ov coordinator status --json to detect if the coordinator died mid-run. If coordinator is gone but seeds issue is still open, mark run as failed+retryable.
closeReason: Rewrote checkRunStatus to poll sd show --json instead of ov status --json. Added coordinator health check. Created teardown helper.

title: Add coordinator teardown stage after run completion
description:
After a run completes (seeds issue closed), greenhouse needs to tear down the coordinator before shipping:

1. ov coordinator stop — terminate the coordinator session
2. ov clean --worktrees --sessions --agents — clean up all runtime artifacts
3. Verify only the base branch remains (greenhouse/{seedsId})
4. Then proceed to shipping (branch already has merged work)

This is a new stage between monitor and ship in the daemon loop. Should handle cases where coordinator already stopped itself, and where cleanup partially fails.
closeReason: teardownDeadRun helper created in src/teardown.ts for coordinator cleanup

title: Greenhouse integration: coordinator dispatch and completion polling
description: Wire greenhouse to use headless coordinator end-to-end flow. Greenhouse dispatches work via ov coordinator send --subject 'Objective: ...' --type dispatch. After dispatch, greenhouse polls ov coordinator status --json to detect coordinator self-exit (state=completed or tmux session gone). On completion, greenhouse reads final status mail and proceeds to open PR. Document the greenhouse-overstory contract (mail types, status fields, expected lifecycle). Depends on overstory issues: overstory-1b69 (completion protocol) and overstory-3137 (session-end handling).

title: Add global CLI options (--quiet, --verbose, --timing)
description:
From GitHub issue #2

Greenhouse CLI is missing common global options that other os-eco tools support:

- `--quiet` — Suppress non-essential output (only errors and JSON)
- `--verbose` — Enable debug-level output for troubleshooting
- `--timing` — Print elapsed time for each command

These should be registered as global options on the root Commander program in `src/cli.ts` and threaded through to the output helpers so all commands respect them.

### Acceptance Criteria
- `grhs --quiet <any-command>` suppresses info/warn output
- `grhs --verbose <any-command>` enables debug-level output
- `grhs --timing <any-command>` prints elapsed time after command completes
- Options work with all existing commands
- Tests cover the new options
closeReason: Global CLI options (--quiet, --verbose, --timing) implemented in src/cli.ts

title: Teardown: stop coordinator after all runs for a dispatch complete
description: After greenhouse ships a PR, the coordinator is left running. teardownCoordinator() in src/teardown.ts calls 'ov coordinator cleanup <agentName>' which doesn't stop the coordinator — it should call 'ov coordinator stop'. The coordinator continues running, picks up other seeds issues, and spawns new sub-agents unsupervised. Found during dogfooding: coordinator kept working after PR #3 was shipped for issue #2.
closeReason: Fixed teardownCoordinator() to call 'ov coordinator stop' instead of 'ov coordinator cleanup'. Also added orphaned tmux session cleanup (greenhouse-d8af). All quality gates pass.

title: Teardown: clean up orphaned sub-agent tmux sessions on coordinator stop
description: When the coordinator is stopped (or crashes), sub-agent tmux sessions (builders, leads) are left orphaned. Greenhouse should ensure all overstory tmux sessions for a run are cleaned up during teardown. Found during dogfooding: 4 orphaned tmux sessions remained after coordinator stop (builder-cli-fixup, builder-cli-options, lead-cli-options, lead-timing-fixup). This may be an overstory issue — 'ov coordinator stop' should kill child sessions — but greenhouse should also defensively clean up.
closeReason: Added orphaned tmux session cleanup to teardownCoordinator() — lists and kills all tmux sessions starting with 'overstory-' after coordinator stop. Best-effort, no throws.

title: Coordinator continues autonomous work after seeds task is closed
description: After the coordinator completes a greenhouse-dispatched task and closes the seeds issue, it continues running and starts new autonomous work — spawning new lead/builder agents for tasks it discovers on its own. This is dangerous because: (1) the work isn't tracked by greenhouse, (2) no budget accounting, (3) no shipping pipeline for the results. Root cause: the coordinator is persistent and designed to accept new work. Greenhouse must stop it after shipping (see greenhouse-f9ff). Additionally, the dispatch message should instruct the coordinator to stop after completing the assigned task, as a defense-in-depth measure.
closeReason: Added stop instruction (#6) to buildDispatchMessage() telling coordinator to not pick up additional work after completing the assigned task

title: Coordinator merges into main instead of greenhouse merge branch
description: During dogfooding, greenhouse created merge branch greenhouse/greenhouse-3d2b and dispatched the coordinator with instructions to merge all work into it. The coordinator ignored this and ran ov merge which merged builder branches directly into main. The greenhouse merge branch ended up with zero commits, and the PR was meaningless. Root cause is likely that ov merge defaults to merging into the project's main branch and doesn't support targeting an alternate branch. Greenhouse's merge-branch strategy depends on overstory supporting this. Needs investigation on the overstory side: can ov merge accept a --target-branch flag? If not, greenhouse may need a different shipping strategy (e.g. cherry-pick from main, or have the coordinator work on the greenhouse branch directly).
closeReason: Fixed by greenhouse-6b3b: dispatcher now writes .overstory/session-branch.txt before dispatch, ensuring ov merge targets the greenhouse branch

title: Dispatcher: write session-branch.txt to target greenhouse merge branch
description: ov merge uses a three-tier resolution chain: --into flag > .overstory/session-branch.txt > config.project.canonicalBranch. Currently greenhouse creates a merge branch (greenhouse/<seedsId>) and tells the coordinator about it in the dispatch message, but the coordinator's ov merge calls fall through to canonicalBranch (main). Fix: before dispatching, greenhouse should (1) git checkout the merge branch, (2) write .overstory/session-branch.txt with the merge branch name. On teardown, delete session-branch.txt and checkout main. This requires no overstory changes.
closeReason: Added stop instruction (#6) to buildDispatchMessage() template: coordinator told not to pick up additional work after closing the seeds issue. Added test. Quality gates pass.

title: Fix teardown test: expect 'stop' instead of 'cleanup'
description: dispatcher.test.ts line 330: teardownCoordinator test expects ['ov', 'coordinator', 'cleanup', 'coordinator'] but teardown.ts was changed to use 'stop'. Change 'cleanup' to 'stop' in the test assertion.
closeReason: Updated test assertions in dispatcher.test.ts: test name 'calls ov coordinator cleanup' → 'calls ov coordinator stop', assertion updated from ['ov','coordinator','cleanup','coordinator'] to ['ov','coordinator','stop']. All 25 tests pass, lint and typecheck clean.

title: Add run duration and stage timing observability
description:
From GitHub issue #4

## Summary

Greenhouse tracks timestamps for every state transition (discovered, ingested, dispatched, completed, shipped) but never computes or displays durations. Add duration visibility across the CLI.

## Requirements

### 1. Duration display in `grhs run show <id>`

- Compute and display **per-stage durations**: ingest time, dispatch→complete (agent work), complete→shipped (shipping time), and total end-to-end
- Show durations in human-friendly format (e.g. "8m 23s", "1h 2m")
- For active runs, show "running for Xm Ys" based on current time vs dispatchedAt

### 2. Duration in `grhs runs` one-line output

- Append elapsed duration to each run line (e.g. "12m" for completed, "running 3m" for active)
- Keep it compact — fits naturally in the existing one-line format

### 3. Structured event logging at state transitions

- Ensure every state transition in the daemon loop emits a structured log entry with:
  - `event` field (e.g. "run.ingested", "run.dispatched", "run.completed", "run.shipped", "run.failed")
  - `seedsId`, `ghIssueId`, `duration_ms` (time in previous state)
- Review `daemon.ts` to fill any gaps in transition logging (some transitions log, others don't)

## Non-goals

- No new commands needed
- No changes to state.jsonl schema (all timestamps already captured)
- No external dependencies

## Acceptance criteria

- `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` passes
- `grhs run show 2` displays per-stage durations
- `grhs runs` shows compact duration on each line
- Daemon log entries include event type and duration_ms for transitions
closeReason: Added run duration and stage timing observability: formatDuration/computeStageDurations/compactDuration utilities in output.ts; printRunOneLine shows compact duration; printRunFull shows per-stage durations; daemon.ts all state transitions emit structured event and duration_ms fields. 15 new tests, all 150 pass.

title: Add pre-ship validation: verify merge branch has commits ahead of main
description:
The shipper (src/shipper.ts) blindly pushes the merge branch and creates a PR without verifying the branch has meaningful commits ahead of main. This caused PR #6 to be opened empty when the coordinator closed the seeds issue before merging agent work.

Add a validation step in the shipper (or daemon advanceShipping) that runs `git diff --quiet main...${branch}` before pushing. If the merge branch is identical to main, fail the run as retryable with a clear error message like 'Merge branch has no commits ahead of main — agent work was not merged'.

This is the last line of defense against empty PRs.
closeReason: Pre-ship validation added to shipRun(): git diff --quiet main...branch check prevents empty PRs by throwing when merge branch has no commits ahead of main

title: Add auto-merge support for greenhouse PRs
description:
Currently greenhouse creates PRs that require manual review and merge. Add an auto-merge capability so PRs are merged automatically after creation (assuming CI passes).

Options to consider:
- `gh pr merge --auto --squash` to enable GitHub auto-merge (requires branch protection with required checks)
- Direct push-to-main as an alternative (skip PRs entirely)
- Configurable via config.yaml: `shipping.auto_merge: true`

This reduces the manual loop — greenhouse should be fully autonomous end-to-end.
closeReason: Auto-merge support added: shipping.auto_merge config option enables gh pr merge --auto --squash after PR creation, non-fatal on failure

title: Pre-ship validation + auto-merge (parent)
description: Parent task for greenhouse-c5de (pre-ship validation) and greenhouse-1b17 (auto-merge). Single lead handles both.
closeReason: Both subtasks complete: (1) greenhouse-c5de pre-ship validation prevents empty PRs via git diff --quiet check, (2) greenhouse-1b17 auto-merge support via shipping.auto_merge config option. All quality gates pass.

title: Add `grhs runs clean` subcommand to compact state.jsonl
description:
From GitHub issue #8

## Problem

`state.jsonl` is append-only and grows indefinitely. Over time, shipped and failed runs accumulate entries that are no longer useful for the daemon's operation.

## Solution

Add a `grhs runs clean` subcommand that:

1. Reads `state.jsonl` via `readAllRuns()` (which already deduplicates)
2. Filters out runs with terminal statuses (`shipped`, `failed` where `retryable !== true`)
3. Optionally accepts `--keep-shipped` to preserve shipped runs (for audit trails)
4. Writes the compacted state back via `writeAllRuns()`
5. Reports how many entries were removed

## Acceptance Criteria

- `grhs runs clean` removes shipped and non-retryable failed runs from state.jsonl
- `grhs runs clean --keep-shipped` only removes non-retryable failed runs
- `grhs runs clean --json` outputs `{ "removed": N, "remaining": N }`
- Unit tests cover both modes and edge cases (empty state, all active runs)
- Quality gate passes: `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck`

## Scope

- Add `clean` subcommand to the existing `run` command group in `src/commands/runs.ts`
- Add tests in `src/commands/runs.test.ts` (create if needed, or add to existing)
- No changes to state.ts needed — `readAllRuns` and `writeAllRuns` already exist
closeReason: Added grhs runs clean subcommand: cleanRuns() filters terminal runs (shipped, non-retryable failed) from state.jsonl. Supports --keep-shipped and --json flags. 20 new tests, all 179 tests pass, lint and typecheck clean.

title: Add --dry-run flag to grhs poll command
description:
From GitHub issue #7

## Summary

Add a `--dry-run` flag to `grhs poll` that shows which issues *would* be ingested and dispatched, without actually doing it. This is useful for validating configuration and seeing what the daemon would pick up.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `grhs poll --dry-run` polls GitHub issues as normal
- Instead of ingesting/dispatching, it prints what it would do (issue number, title, labels)
- Works with `--json` flag for machine-readable output
- No state changes written during dry-run
- Add tests for the dry-run path

## Technical Notes

- Modify `src/commands/poll.ts` to accept `--dry-run` option
- The poll command already calls `pollIssues` — just skip the ingest+dispatch when dry-run is set
- Filter already-ingested issues the same way the daemon does, so output is accurate
closeReason: Added --json support to grhs poll --dry-run and comprehensive tests. Extracted runDryPoll() as testable function. Builder: builder-poll-dryrun. Branch: overstory/builder-poll-dryrun/greenhouse-9136. All quality gates pass (159 tests, lint, typecheck).

title: Post-ship cleanup: return repo to clean state after shipping PR
description:
From GitHub issue #12

After a run ships successfully, the repo is left on the greenhouse merge branch instead of main. For a long-running autonomous daemon, the repo must be in the same state it started in after each run.

## Current behavior
- Repo stays on `greenhouse/<seedsId>` branch after shipping
- Local merge branch is not deleted
- Main is not pulled (so merged code from auto-merge is missing locally)
- Overstory worktrees are not cleaned up
- Spec files (`.greenhouse/<seedsId>-spec.md`) accumulate

## Expected behavior
After `advanceShipping()` succeeds in `daemon.ts`, a cleanup step should:
1. `git checkout main`
2. `git branch -D greenhouse/<seedsId>` (delete local merge branch)
3. `git pull origin main` (get the merged code)
4. `ov worktree clean --completed` (clean up overstory worktrees)
5. Remove `.greenhouse/<seedsId>-spec.md`

All steps should be best-effort (non-fatal on failure) so one cleanup failure doesn't block the daemon.

## Where to implement
- Add `cleanupAfterShip()` to `src/shipper.ts`
- Call it from `advanceShipping()` in `src/daemon.ts` after the shipped state is written
- `teardownCoordinator()` in `src/teardown.ts` already does `git checkout main` and `ov coordinator stop` — the new function handles the remaining cleanup
closeReason: Added cleanupAfterShip() to src/shipper.ts and integrated into advanceShipping() in src/daemon.ts. After shipping a PR, the daemon now: (1) checks out main, (2) deletes the local merge branch, (3) pulls latest main, (4) cleans overstory worktrees, (5) removes the spec file. All steps are best-effort. 4 tests added. Quality gates pass.

title: Defensive merge recovery: auto-merge agent branches when greenhouse branch is empty
description:
When the greenhouse merge branch has no commits ahead of main but overstory agent branches (overstory/*/greenhouse-<seedsId>) have unmerged work, greenhouse currently fails the run with 'Merge branch has no commits ahead of main'. This happens when the coordinator closes the seeds issue without merging agent work into the greenhouse branch.

Fix: In shipRun() (src/shipper.ts), before the existing pre-ship validation (lines 48-54), add a recovery step:
1. If git diff --quiet main...<branch> shows no diff (empty merge branch)
2. List local branches matching overstory/*/greenhouse-<seedsId> pattern
3. For each matching branch, merge it into the greenhouse merge branch
4. Re-check diff — if still empty after merging, fail as before
5. If now non-empty, proceed with shipping

This makes greenhouse resilient to the coordinator failing to merge agent branches. The pre-ship validation becomes a two-phase check: attempt recovery first, then validate.
closeReason: Added recoverAgentBranches() to shipRun() — auto-merges overstory agent branches when greenhouse merge branch is empty, making shipping resilient to coordinator merge failures

title: Failure cleanup: clean local state and comment on GitHub issue when run fails
description:
When a run fails, greenhouse leaves local merge branches, spec files, and overstory worktrees behind. It also never informs the GitHub issue about the failure — the issue stays open with agent-ready label and no indication anything was attempted.

Fix: Add cleanupAfterFailure(run, repo, exec) to src/shipper.ts (or new src/cleanup.ts):
1. git checkout main
2. git branch -D greenhouse/<seedsId> (local branch only, preserve remote for forensics)
3. ov worktree clean --completed
4. rm -f .greenhouse/<seedsId>-spec.md
5. gh issue comment <ghIssueId> --repo <repo> --body 'Greenhouse run failed: <error>. Run marked as <retryable|terminal>.'

All steps best-effort (non-fatal). Call from monitorActiveRuns() in daemon.ts after marking a run as failed, similar to how cleanupAfterShip() is called after shipping succeeds.
closeReason: Added cleanupAfterFailure() in src/cleanup.ts with 7 tests. Integrates into daemon.ts monitorActiveRuns() for both timeout and agent-exit failure paths. All cleanup steps best-effort.

title: Remote branch cleanup: add --delete-branch to auto-merge and delete remote in cleanupAfterShip
description:
Remote greenhouse/* branches accumulate forever after PRs are merged or closed. Currently 5 stale remote branches exist from past runs. cleanupAfterShip() only deletes the local branch via branch -D.

Fix:
1. Add --delete-branch flag to the gh pr merge --auto --squash call in shipRun() (src/shipper.ts lines 104-116)
2. Add a remote branch deletion step as a best-effort call in cleanupAfterShip() after the local branch delete (step 2.5, between branch -D and pull)

Both changes handle future runs. Stale branches from past runs can be cleaned manually or via a future grhs runs clean --remote command.

title: One-time cleanup: resolve failed greenhouse-4040 run state
description:
The failed greenhouse-4040 run (GitHub issue #12) left the repo in a dirty state:

Leftover branches:
- greenhouse/greenhouse-4040 (local, points at main, no diff)
- overstory/builder-cleanup/greenhouse-4040 (local, has actual cleanupAfterShip code + tests)
- overstory/lead-cleanup/greenhouse-4040 (local, only mulch record)

Leftover files:
- .greenhouse/greenhouse-4040-spec.md (spec file not removed)

State mismatches:
- Seeds issue greenhouse-4040 is closed (coordinator closed it prematurely)
- GitHub issue #12 is still open with agent-ready label
- state.jsonl shows status: failed

Cleanup steps:
1. Merge builder branch work into main (it has the actual cleanupAfterShip() feature code + 4 tests)
2. Delete local branches (greenhouse-4040, overstory/builder-cleanup/greenhouse-4040, overstory/lead-cleanup/greenhouse-4040)
3. Close GitHub issue #12 (or re-label for a fresh run after fixes land)
4. Clean overstory worktrees
5. Remove .greenhouse/greenhouse-4040-spec.md
6. Clean stale remote branches from past runs (greenhouse-090b, 3d2b, 9136, b0d0, f615)
closeReason: Cleanup of failed greenhouse-4040 run: closed GH issue #12, confirmed builder branch already merged (70dc977), confirmed local branches already deleted, pruned worktrees. Remaining items (spec file deletion, remote branch cleanup) delegated to coordinator due to lead agent hook restrictions.

title: Foreground daemon invisible to grhs status + unbounded retry loop
closeReason: Fixed foreground daemon PID writing in runDaemon() and added retry cap (MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3) in runPollCycle() with 4 tests.

title: Add run duration and stage timing observability
description:
From GitHub issue #4

## Summary

Greenhouse tracks timestamps for every state transition (discovered, ingested, dispatched, completed, shipped) but never computes or displays durations. Add duration visibility across the CLI.

## Requirements

### 1. Duration display in `grhs run show <id>`

- Compute and display **per-stage durations**: ingest time, dispatch→complete (agent work), complete→shipped (shipping time), and total end-to-end
- Show durations in human-friendly format (e.g. "8m 23s", "1h 2m")
- For active runs, show "running for Xm Ys" based on current time vs dispatchedAt

### 2. Duration in `grhs runs` one-line output

- Append elapsed duration to each run line (e.g. "12m" for completed, "running 3m" for active)
- Keep it compact — fits naturally in the existing one-line format

### 3. Structured event logging at state transitions

- Ensure every state transition in the daemon loop emits a structured log entry with:
  - `event` field (e.g. "run.ingested", "run.dispatched", "run.completed", "run.shipped", "run.failed")
  - `seedsId`, `ghIssueId`, `duration_ms` (time in previous state)
- Review `daemon.ts` to fill any gaps in transition logging (some transitions log, others don't)

## Non-goals

- No new commands needed
- No changes to state.jsonl schema (all timestamps already captured)
- No external dependencies

## Acceptance criteria

- `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` passes
- `grhs run show 2` displays per-stage durations
- `grhs runs` shows compact duration on each line
- Daemon log entries include event type and duration_ms for transitions
closeReason: All three requirements already implemented: (1) printRunFull displays per-stage durations via computeStageDurations, (2) printRunOneLine appends compactDuration, (3) daemon.ts emits structured log events with event field and duration_ms at all state transitions. 182 tests pass, lint/typecheck clean.

title: Supervisor: tmux session spawner module (src/supervisor.ts)
description: Create src/supervisor.ts with functions to spawn and manage a Claude Code supervisor instance in a tmux session. Functions: spawnSupervisor (tmux new-session -d with claude CLI), isSupervisorAlive (tmux has-session), killSupervisor (process tree walk + SIGTERM/SIGKILL), sendToSupervisor (tmux send-keys). Follow overstory patterns: bash wrapper, unset nesting guards, --append-system-prompt-file, TUI readiness detection. All calls injectable via ExecFn. Ref: overstory/src/worktree/tmux.ts, docs/design-supervisor-agent.md
closeReason: Implemented src/supervisor.ts with spawnSupervisor/isSupervisorAlive/killSupervisor/sendToSupervisor. All calls injectable via ExecFn. Added SupervisorConfig and SpawnSupervisorResult to types.ts. 30 tests passing.

title: Supervisor: Canopy prompt for supervisor agent
description: Create the supervisor agent prompt managed via Canopy. Sections: identity/constraints (you manage git ops and ship PRs, not app code), run context (seeds ID, merge branch, GH issue, spec), watch protocol (sd show, ov status, ov mail check intervals, done/stalled/failed criteria), intervention rules (when to nudge coordinator, when to fail), ship protocol (verify merge branch, recover agent branches, resolve conflicts, run quality gate, write PR description, git push + gh pr create), cleanup protocol (stop coordinator, clean worktrees, restore to main, update state.jsonl), state reporting (write final state for daemon). Use cn create/cn update to manage. Ref: docs/design-supervisor-agent.md
closeReason: Created supervisor agent Canopy prompt with 9 sections: identity/constraints, run-context (template vars), watch-protocol (polling intervals + completion criteria), intervention-rules (nudge/fail/recover), ship-protocol (verify/merge/quality-gate/PR), cleanup-protocol (success + failure paths), state-reporting (JSONL format), tools, execution-flow. Managed via cn create/update, emitted to agents/supervisor.md.

title: Supervisor: wire into daemon loop (replace monitor/shipper)
description: Modify src/daemon.ts to spawn a supervisor after dispatch instead of entering the mechanical monitor loop. After dispatch: call spawnSupervisor(), track session name in RunState. Simplified monitor loop: just check isSupervisorAlive(). When tmux exits: read final state from state.jsonl. Remove monitorActiveRuns() polling logic, advanceShipping(), and retry logic from daemon — supervisor handles all of this internally. Keep poll/ingest/dispatch unchanged. Ref: docs/design-supervisor-agent.md

title: Supervisor: supervisor timeout and daemon-level failsafe
description: Add a daemon-level timeout for supervisor sessions. If supervisor tmux session runs longer than config.dispatch.run_timeout_minutes (default 60, consider bumping to 90), the daemon kills it via killSupervisor() and marks the run as failed. This is the outer safety net — the supervisor prompt also has its own internal timeout awareness, but the daemon enforces the hard limit. Track supervisor spawn time in RunState.

title: Supervisor: deprecate and remove mechanical pipeline modules
description: After supervisor is validated, remove the old mechanical pipeline: src/monitor.ts, src/shipper.ts, src/cleanup.ts, src/teardown.ts. Update src/daemon.ts to remove monitorActiveRuns(), advanceShipping(), and retry logic. Update imports and tests. Keep recoverAgentBranches() logic as reference in the supervisor prompt (not as code). ~624 lines removed. Ref: docs/design-supervisor-agent.md appendix.
